_readme.what_this_is: Archetype MANIFEST for a personal / portfolio / content site. Resolve with `node library/resolve.mjs archetype.portfolio.json`.
_readme.reuse: Still 100% composed from the shared library — ZERO portfolio-specific checkpoints. The design.* checkpoints are the same ones the ecommerce archetype uses; the web.* checkpoints are shared with any content site; and it now also draws the universal security.*/data.*/reliability.* checkpoints every deployed site needs (HTTPS + headers, secrets, PII on the contact form, injection, error handling) — auth/upload/query checkpoints are omitted since a portfolio has no accounts or backend to speak of. The strongest demonstration of the library: a whole archetype with nothing new to author.
_readme.no_criticals: A portfolio has no revenue/data-critical backbone, so its goal_definition is expressed in terms of severity:high (the design + performance bar) rather than critical. goal_definition is archetype-contextual, like severity.
_readme.how_scoring_works: Levels 3/6/9. 3 = crude/risky. 6 = solid, shippable. 9 = great. TRUST THE GAP, NOT THE NUMBER.

applies_when: A personal, portfolio, or content site: mostly static content, a high design bar, an optional contact form, little or no backend state.

goal_definition.honest_ceiling: design checkpoints tagged taste_limited verify to ~level 7 mechanically; reaching 9 requires human design review the rubric cannot replace.
